summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering bill payments, out-of-town travel approvals for staff training, acceptance of a resignation, hiring of five part-time firefighter/EMTs, and appointment of a part-time service worker. Chief Meyer reported on a medic vehicle crash, coverage continuity, and announced an American Heart Association Mission Lifeline EMS award. An executive session was held to discuss personnel matters, after which a zoning inspector discussion and a part-time service worker appointment were addressed.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (June 27 – July 10, 2023) – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Jen Farmer to attend first responder mental health and wellness training in Columbus, OH (9/7–9/8) at $325 registration – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Michaela Young to attend State Personnel Board of Review Training Conference in Columbus, OH (8/10/23) at $175 registration – passed
Motion to accept a resignation (details partially garbled) – passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Jeff Scott effective 7/16 at $15.50/hr with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Lauren Gray effective 7/16 at $15.50/hr with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Connor Brown effective 7/16 at $15.50/hr with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Nathan Crowley effective 7/16 at $15.50/hr with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Lucas Kroner effective 7/16 at $15.50/hr with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to adjourn to Executive Session at 6:13 PM per ORC 121.22 – passed
Motion to appoint James Pruitt as part-time service worker at $17/hr with one-year probationary period contingent on pre-employment screening – passed
Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 7:34 PM – passed
action_items: Jen Farmer to attend first responder mental health and wellness training in Columbus, OH (Sept 7-8)
Michaela Young to attend State Personnel Board of Review Training Conference in Columbus, OH (Aug 10)
Chief Meyer / Administration to onboard five new part-time firefighter/EMTs effective 7/16/23
Administration to complete pre-employment screening for James Pruitt before starting as part-time service worker
Administration to continue work on filling the zoning inspector position
key_amounts: $325 – registration cost for Jen Farmer's mental health and wellness training
$175 – registration cost for Michaela Young's State Personnel Board of Review Training Conference
$15.50/hr – pay rate for five new part-time firefighter/EMTs (Scott, Gray, Brown, Crowley, Kroner)
$17/hr – pay rate for part-time service worker James Pruitt
notable_topics: Medic vehicle crash incident and continuity of EMS coverage
American Heart Association Mission Lifeline EMS award received
First responder mental health and wellness training
Hiring of five part-time firefighter/EMTs to replace departing personnel
Appointment of part-time service worker James Pruitt
Lions Club Fourth of July flag display recognition
notes: Significant portions of the transcript are garbled or contain nonsensical text, likely due to automated transcription errors. A medic unit was involved in a crash the day prior but no interruption in coverage occurred. The executive session ran from approximately 6:13 PM to 7:33 PM. Station 18 update was mentioned but details were lost in transcript corruption.
title: BOT Meeting 7-11-23
